prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Warhammer Fantasy Old World inspired grimdark low-fantasy RPG setting. Terrain type: diverse_archipelago. Draw an entire grim old-world fantasy map made of many irregular island chains and scattered landmasses, unevenly distributed across the page, in dark fantasy parchment cartography. The islands must vary strongly in size and shape: broad empire province islands, crescent blighted marsh islands, tiny reef specks, long dwarf mountain ridge islands, northern Chaos waste islands, black forest islands, plague harbor islands, greenskin rocks, haunted barrow isles, cursed fortress islands, pirate coves, witch coves, and storm-lashed ruin chains. Include multiple climates and biomes across the archipelago: temperate river provinces, dark pine forests, snowy northern wastes, alpine mountains, cursed marshes, cold steppe, blasted Chaos badlands, desolate moors, storm coasts, and polluted river deltas. Coastlines on every major island should be realistic and complex with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, deltas, fjords, lagoons, barrier islands, sea caves, reefs, shoals, and hidden coves. Make the island distribution asymmetrical and natural, with dense cursed clusters in some regions and wide dangerous seas elsewhere. Large detailed grimdark atlas, oppressive palette, sinister wilderness markers allowed, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
